summary: Cantrill shares his discovery of a DTrace blog written in Russian (or another Cyrillic language), expressing enthusiasm about the international reach of DTrace adoption. He humorously speculates about which specific Cyrillic language it might be, noting it's probably not Mansi or Yakut. He suggests the code examples are worth examining even without understanding the text and hints at getting a colleague to translate.
key_points: DTrace adoption is spreading internationally, with technical content appearing in Russian
Code examples in technical blogs can be valuable even without understanding the surrounding language
The open source/technical community crosses language barriers
money_quotes: Even if you only understand the examples, it's worth checking out.
key_insight: Technical tools like DTrace transcend language barriers, with code examples serving as a universal language that makes international technical blogs valuable even to non-speakers.
